title: What Is SXO? — Search Experience Optimization Defined
meta_description: Search Experience Optimization (SXO) governs the full journey from search visibility to user action. It is not a shallow combination of SEO, UX, and CRO.
canonical_intent: Establish the canonical definition of SXO, making SuperSXO.com the authoritative reference for the discipline.
summary: This page defines Search Experience Optimization with precision and authority. It distinguishes SXO from surface-level combinations of SEO and UX, and frames SXO as the governed control layer between search visibility and user decision.

required_sections.name: definition
required_sections.purpose: Provide the precise, doctrine-grade definition of SXO. Must be distinct from shallow 'SEO + UX' descriptions.

required_sections.name: why_seo_alone_fails
required_sections.purpose: Explain that ranking is a necessary but insufficient condition for digital success.

required_sections.name: sxo_as_governed_layer
required_sections.purpose: Frame SXO as the governed discipline between search visibility and user decision — not a tactic stack.

required_sections.name: journey_overview
required_sections.purpose: Briefly introduce the search-to-action journey layers. Lead to /sxo-framework/ for depth.

required_sections.name: sxo_vs_seo_pointer
required_sections.purpose: Direct toward /seo-vs-sxo/ for the full comparison without duplicating its content.

blocked_claims: SXO is the same as CRO.
SXO is a new marketing term.
editorial_notes: This page must be reference-grade, not blog-style. Definitions must be precise. Do not position SXO as a trend or buzzword. Avoid repetition of content from /sxo-framework/. Write as institutional doctrine.
publication_blockers: Route status must be 'published' in data/routes.json.
Definition copy must be reviewed and claim-classified.
All internal links must resolve to published routes.
